title: sorbus aria var. decaisneana
intro: Sorbus aria var. decaisneana, also known as the Whitebeam, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe, belonging to the Rosaceae family. The medicinal properties of Whitebeam lie in its bark and leaves, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and fever. The plant's health benefits include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, wounds, and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, Whitebeam is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be taken to promote overall well-being and support the body's natural healing processes.
